sectiondef.header: Address mapping for the base EDID block.

briefdescription.para: Vendor/product ID manufacturer name byte 0 offset.
Vendor/product ID manufacturer name byte 1 offset.
Vendor/product ID product code LSB offset.
Vendor/product ID product code MSB offset.
Vendor/product ID serial number byte 0 offset.
Vendor/product ID serial number byte 1 offset.
Vendor/product ID serial number byte 2 offset.
Vendor/product ID serial number byte 3 offset.
Vendor/product ID week of manufacture offset.
Vendor/product ID year of manufacture/model offset.
Basic display video input definition offset.
Basic display horizontal screen size or aspect ratio offset.
Basic display vertical screen size or aspect ratio offset.
Color characteristics red/green low bits offset.
Color characteristics blue/white low bits offset.
Color characteristics red X high bits offset.
Color characteristics red Y high bits offset.
Color characteristics green X high bits offset.
Standard timing aspect ratio and field refresh rate offset (N=1-8)
DTD/PTM pixel clock in kHz LSB offset.
DTD/PTM pixel clock in kHz MSB offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal addressable pixels LSB offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal blanking pixels LSB offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal res/blanking upper 4 bits offset.
DTD/PTM vertical addressable lines LSB offset.
DTD/PTM vertical blanking lines LSB offset.
DTD/PTM vertical res/blanking upper 4 bits offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal front porch pixels LSB offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal sync pulse width LSB offset.
DTD/PTM vertical front porch and sync pulse width lower 4 bits offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal/vertical front porch and sync pulse width upper 2 bits offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal display size in mm LSB offset.
DTD/PTM vertical display size in mm LSB offset.
DTD/PTM horizontal/vertical display size upper 4 bits offset.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Masks, shifts, and values.

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics green Y high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics green Y high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics green Y high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics green Y high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics blue X high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics blue X high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics blue X high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics blue X high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics blue Y high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics blue Y high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics blue Y high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics blue Y high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics white X high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics white point X high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics white X high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics white point X high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics white Y high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics white point Y high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Color characteristics white Y high bits offset.

detaileddescription.para: Color characteristics white point Y high bits offset (duplicate definition)

briefdescription.para: Manufacturer name character 1 low bits mask.
Manufacturer name character 1 high bits shift.
Analog signal level standard bit shift.
Analog signal level standard bit mask.
Analog blank-to-black setup expected bit mask.
Analog separate sync H and V signals supported bit mask.
Analog composite sync signal on H bit mask.
Analog composite sync signal on green bit mask.
Analog serration on V sync pulse bit mask.
Digital bits per color bit shift.
Digital bits per color bit mask.
Digital video interface standard bit mask.
Feature support standby power management bit mask.
Feature support suspend power management bit mask.
Feature support active-off/very low power bit mask.
Feature support analog display color type bit shift.
Feature support analog display color type bit mask.
Analog display color type RGB color.
Analog display color type non-RGB color.
Feature support digital YCrCb 4:4:4 color encoding bit mask.
Feature support digital YCrCb 4:2:2 color encoding bit mask.
Feature support sRGB is default color space bit mask.
Feature support preferred timing mode included bit mask.
Feature support continuous frequency operation bit mask.
Color characteristics high bits shift value.
Red/Blue X coordinate low bits shift.
Red/Blue Y coordinate low bits shift.
Red/Blue Y coordinate low bits mask.
Green/White X coordinate low bits shift.
Green/White X coordinate low bits mask.
Green/White Y coordinate low bits mask.
Standard timings aspect ratio bit shift.
Standard timings field refresh rate bit mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal/vertical resolution and blanking upper 4 bits: blanking mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal/vertical resolution and blanking upper 4 bits: resolution mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal/vertical resolution and blanking upper 4 bits: resolution shift.
DTD/PTM vertical front porch and sync pulse width lower 4 bits: sync pulse width mask.
DTD/PTM vertical front porch and sync pulse width lower 4 bits: front porch mask.
DTD/PTM vertical front porch and sync pulse width lower 4 bits: front porch shift.
DTD/PTM horizontal front porch upper 2 bits mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal sync pulse width upper 2 bits mask.
DTD/PTM vertical front porch upper 2 bits mask.
DTD/PTM vertical sync pulse width upper 2 bits mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal front porch upper 2 bits shift.
DTD/PTM horizontal sync pulse width upper 2 bits shift.
DTD/PTM vertical front porch upper 2 bits shift.
DTD/PTM vertical image size upper 4 bits mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal image size upper 4 bits mask.
DTD/PTM horizontal image size upper 4 bits shift.
This typedef enumerates the list of available standard display monitor timings as specified in the xvidc_timings_table.c file.

detaileddescription.para: The naming format is:
XVIDC_VM_<RESOLUTION>_<REFRESH RATE (HZ)>_<P|I>(_RB)
Where RB stands for reduced blanking.

briefdescription.para: Color depth - bits per color component.
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (10-bit LSB Aligned)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (10-bit LSB Aligned, 4:2:0)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (12-bit LSB Aligned)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (12-bit LSB Aligned, 4:2:0)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (10-bit MSB Aligned)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (10-bit MSB Aligned, 4:2:0)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (12-bit MSB Aligned)
Memory format [31:0] Y1:Y0 16:16 [31:0] V0:U0 16:16 (12-bit MSB Aligned, 4:2:0)
Number of supported color formats (includes reserved slots)

detaileddescription.para: Based on CTA861-G

briefdescription.para: Callback type which represents a custom timer wait handler.

detaileddescription.para: This is only used for Microblaze since it doesn't have a native sleep function. To avoid dependency on a hardware timer, the default wait functionality is implemented using loop iterations; this isn't too accurate. Therefore a custom timer handler is used, the user may implement their own wait implementation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the timer instance.
is the duration (msec/usec) to be passed to the timer function.

briefdescription.para: This table contains the main stream attributes for various standard resolutions.

detaileddescription.para: Each entry is of the format: 1) ID: XVIDC_VM_<HRES>x<VRES>_<FRAME RATE (HZ)>_<P|I>(RB = Reduced Blanking) 2) Resolution naming: "<HRES>x<VRES>@<FRAME RATE (HZ)>" 3) Frame rate: XVIDC_FR<FRAME RATE (HZ)> 4) Video timing structure: 1) Horizontal active resolution (pixels) 2) Horizontal front porch (pixels) 3) Horizontal sync width (pixels) 4) Horizontal back porch (pixels) 5) Horizontal total (pixels) 6) Horizontal sync polarity (0=negative|1=positive) 7) Vertical active resolution (lines) 8) Frame 0: Vertical front porch (lines) 9) Frame 0: Vertical sync width (lines) 10) Frame 0: Vertical back porch (lines) 11) Frame 0: Vertical total (lines) 12) Frame 1: Vertical front porch (lines) 13) Frame 1: Vertical sync width (lines) 14) Frame 1: Vertical back porch (lines) 15) Frame 1: Vertical total (lines) 16) Vertical sync polarity (0=negative|1=positive)

briefdescription.para: This table contains the main stream attributes for various standard resolutions.

detaileddescription.para: Each entry is of the format: 1) ID: XVIDC_VM_<HRES>x<VRES>_<FRAME RATE (HZ)>_<P|I>(RB = Reduced Blanking) 2) Resolution naming: "<HRES>x<VRES>@<FRAME RATE (HZ)>" 3) Frame rate: XVIDC_FR<FRAME RATE (HZ)> 4) Video timing structure: 1) Horizontal active resolution (pixels) 2) Horizontal front porch (pixels) 3) Horizontal sync width (pixels) 4) Horizontal back porch (pixels) 5) Horizontal total (pixels) 6) Horizontal sync polarity (0=negative|1=positive) 7) Vertical active resolution (lines) 8) Frame 0: Vertical front porch (lines) 9) Frame 0: Vertical sync width (lines) 10) Frame 0: Vertical back porch (lines) 11) Frame 0: Vertical total (lines) 12) Frame 1: Vertical front porch (lines) 13) Frame 1: Vertical sync width (lines) 14) Frame 1: Vertical back porch (lines) 15) Frame 1: Vertical total (lines) 16) Vertical sync polarity (0=negative|1=positive)

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to video mode data at the provided index of the custom video mode table.

simplesect.para: Pointer to XVidC_VideoTimingMode structure based on the given video mode.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns whether or not the video timing mode is a reduced blanking mode or not.

parameterdescription.para:
specifies the type of reduced blanking:
0 = No Reduced Blanking
1 = RB
2 = RB2

simplesect.para:
If the reduced blanking type is compatible with the video mode:
0 = Not supported
1 = Video mode supports the RB type
None.

briefdescription.para: This function registers a user-defined custom video mode timing table with video_common.

detaileddescription.para: Functions which search the available video modes, or take VmId as an input, will operate on or check the custom video mode timing table in addition to the pre-defined video mode timing table (XVidC_VideoTimingModes).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the user-defined custom vide mode timing table to register.
is the number of video modes supported by CustomTable.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the custom table was successfully registered.
XST_FAILURE if an existing custom table is already present.
IDs in the custom table may not conflict with IDs reserved by the XVidC_VideoMode enum.

briefdescription.para: This function unregisters the user-defined custom video mode timing table previously registered by XVidC_RegisterCustomTimingModes().

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates pixel clock based on the inputs.

parameterdescription.para: specifies rate at which frames are generated.

simplesect.para: Pixel clock in Hz.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates pixel clock from video mode.

simplesect.para: Pixel clock in Hz.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the input video mode is interlaced/progressive based on its ID from the video timings table.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the resolution ID from the video timings table.

simplesect.para:
Video format.
XVIDC_VF_PROGRESSIVE
XVIDC_VF_INTERLACED
None.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the input video mode is interlaced based on its ID from the video timings table.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the resolution ID from the video timings table.

simplesect.para: 1 if the video timing with the supplied table ID is interlaced; 0 if the video timing is progressive.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Video Mode ID that matches the detected input timing, frame rate and I/P flag.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to timing parameters to match

simplesect.para: Id of a supported video mode.
This is an extension of XVidC_GetVideoModeId API to include blanking information in match process. No attempt is made to search for reduced blanking entries, if any.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 Width, u32 Height, u32 FrameRate, u8 IsInterlaced)
memberdef.name: XVidC_GetVideoModeId

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Video Mode ID that matches the detected input width, height, frame rate and I/P flag.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the number pixels per scanline.

simplesect.para: Id of a supported video mode.
None.

memberdef.argsstring: (XVidC_VideoTiming *Timing, u32 FrameRate, u8 IsInterlaced, u8 IsExtensive)
memberdef.name: XVidC_GetVideoModeIdExtensive

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Video Mode ID that matches the detected input timing, frame rate and I/P flag.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to timing parameters to match

is flag.
0 = Basic matching of timing parameters
1 = Extensive matching of timing parameters

simplesect.para: Id of a supported video mode.
This function attempts to search for reduced blanking entries, if any.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 Width, u32 Height, u32 FrameRate, u8 IsInterlaced, u8 RbN)
memberdef.name: XVidC_GetVideoModeIdRb

briefdescription.para: This function returns the video mode ID that matches the detected input width, height, frame rate, interlaced or progressive, and reduced blanking.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the number pixels per scanline.

specifies interlaced or progressive mode:
0 = Progressive
1 = Interlaced.

specifies the type of reduced blanking:
0 = No reduced blanking
1 = RB
2 = RB2

simplesect.para: ID of a supported video mode.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the pointer to video mode data at index provided.

simplesect.para: Pointer to XVidC_VideoTimingMode structure based on the given video mode.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the resolution name for index specified.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a resolution name string.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the frame rate name for index specified.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a frame rate name string.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a string representation of the enumerated type, XVidC_3DFormat.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the converted string.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the color format name for index specified.

parameterdescription.para: specifies the index of color format space.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a color space name string.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the frame rate for index specified.

simplesect.para: Frame rate in Hz.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the timing parameters for specified resolution.

simplesect.para: Pointer to a XVidC_VideoTiming structure.
None.

memberdef.argsstring: (XVidC_VideoStream *VidStrmPtr, XVidC_VideoMode VmId, XVidC_ColorFormat ColorFormat, XVidC_ColorDepth Bpc, XVidC_PixelsPerClock Ppc)
memberdef.name: XVidC_SetVideoStream

briefdescription.para: This function sets the VideoStream structure for the specified video format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XVidC_VideoStream structure to be set.
specifies the color depth/bits per color component.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the timing for the supplied ID was found.
XST_FAILURE, otherwise.
None.

memberdef.argsstring: (XVidC_VideoStream *VidStrmPtr, XVidC_VideoMode VmId, XVidC_ColorFormat ColorFormat, XVidC_ColorDepth Bpc, XVidC_PixelsPerClock Ppc, XVidC_3DInfo *Info3DPtr)
memberdef.name: XVidC_Set3DVideoStream

briefdescription.para: This function sets the VideoStream structure for the specified 3D video format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XVidC_VideoStream structure to be set.
specifies the color depth/bits per color component.
is a pointer to a XVidC_3DInfo structure.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the timing for the supplied ID was found.
XST_FAILURE, otherwise.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the stream information on STDIO/UART console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to video stream.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: This function prints timing information on STDIO/Uart console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to Video Timing structure of the stream.
is a TRUE/FALSE flag that denotes the timing parameter is for interlaced/progressive stream.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether or not a specified video timing mode is the preferred timing of the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to check the timing against.
is the video timing mode to check for support.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the video timing mode is the preferred timing as specified in the base EDID.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.
None.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether or not a specified video timing mode is supported in the established timings field of the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to check the timing against.
is the video timing mode to check for support.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the video timing mode is supported in the base EDID's established timings field.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.
None.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether or not a specified video timing mode is supported in the standard timings field of the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to check the timing against.
is the video timing mode to check for support.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the video timing mode is supported in the base EDID's standard timings fields.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.
None.

parameterdescription.para: is b in the power operation, b^n.
is n in the power operation, b^n.

simplesect.para: Base^Power (Base to the power of Power).
None.

briefdescription.para: Convert a fractional binary number into a fixed point Q0.DecPtIndex number Binary digits to the right of the decimal point represent 2^-1 to 2^-(DecPtIndex+1).

detaileddescription.para: Binary digits to the left of the decimal point represent 2^0, 2^1, etc. For a given Q format, using an unsigned integer container with n fractional bits: its range is [0, 2^-n] its resolution is 2^n

parameterdescription.para: is the binary representation of the fraction.

is the index of the decimal point in the binary number. The decimal point is between the binary digits at Val's indices (DecPtIndex -1) and (DecPtIndex). DecPtIndex will determine the Q format resolution.

simplesect.para: Fixed point representation of the fractional part of the binary number in Q format.
None.

briefdescription.para: Get the manufacturer name as specified in the vendor and product ID field of the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve the manufacturer name from.
is the string that will be modified to hold the retrieved manufacturer name.

simplesect.para: The ManName argument is modified with the manufacturer name.

briefdescription.para: Get the color bit depth (bits per primary color) as specified in the basic display parameters and features, video input definition field of the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve color depth information from.

simplesect.para: The number of bits per primary color as specified by the supplied base EDID.
None.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the x chromaticity coordinate for red by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The x chromatacity coordinate for red.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the y chromaticity coordinate for red by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The y chromatacity coordinate for red.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the x chromaticity coordinate for green by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The x chromatacity coordinate for green.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the y chromaticity coordinate for green by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The y chromatacity coordinate for green.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the x chromaticity coordinate for blue by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The x chromatacity coordinate for blue.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the y chromaticity coordinate for blue by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The y chromatacity coordinate for blue.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the x chromaticity coordinate for white by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to a integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The x chromatacity coordinate for white.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the y chromaticity coordinate for white by converting a 10 bit binary fraction representation from the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) to an integer in Q0.10 Format.

detaileddescription.para: To convert back to float divide the fixed point value by 2^10.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to retrieve chromaticity information from.

simplesect.para: The y chromatacity coordinate for white.
All values will be accurate to +/-0.0005.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the active vertical resolution from the standard timings field of the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to check the timing against.
specifies which one of the standard timings to retrieve from the standard timings field.

simplesect.para: The vertical active resolution of the specified standard timing from the supplied base EDID.
StdTimingsNum is an index 1-8.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether or not a specified video timing mode is supported as specified in the supplied base Extended Display Identification Data (EDID).

detaileddescription.para: The preferred timing, established timings (I, II, II), and the standard timings fields are checked for support.

parameterdescription.para: is the supplied base EDID to check the timing against.
is the video timing mode to check for support.

simplesect.para:
Status code.
XST_SUCCESS if the video timing mode is supported as specified in the supplied base EDID.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.
None.

briefdescription.para: This macro check if video stream is 3D or 2D.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XVidC_VideoStream structure.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u8 XDp_IsStream3D(XVidC_VideoStream *VidStreamPtr)

briefdescription.para: Check if EDID header matches valid signature.
Get week of manufacture from EDID.
Check if year field represents model year (week=0xFF)
Check if year field represents manufacture year (week!=0xFF)
Check if display uses digital signal input.
Check if display uses analog signal input.
Check if analog separate sync H and V supported.
Check if analog composite sync on H supported.
Check if analog composite sync on G (green) supported.
Check if analog serration on V sync pulse supported.
Get digital signal video interface standard.
Check if screen size or aspect ratio is defined.
Get horizontal screen size or aspect ratio value.
Get vertical screen size or aspect ratio value.
Check if values represent screen size (both non-zero)
Check if values represent landscape aspect ratio (H non-zero, V zero)
Check if values represent portrait aspect ratio (H zero, V non-zero)
Check if gamma value is stored in extension block.
Check if standby power management supported.
Check if suspend power management supported.
Check if active-off/very low power supported.
Check if digital YCrCb 4:4:4 color encoding supported.
Check if digital YCrCb 4:2:2 color encoding supported.
Check if sRGB is default color space.
Check if preferred timing mode included.
Check if continuous frequency operation supported.
Get standard timing field refresh rate.
Check if DTD/PTM uses interlaced scan.
Get number of EDID extension blocks.
Contains common utility functions that are typically used by video-related drivers and applications.

detaileddescription.para: Contains video timings for various standard resolutions.
Contains macros, definitions, and function declarations related to the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) structure which is present in all monitors.
Contains function definitions related to the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) structure which is present in all monitors.

simplesect.para: None.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 rc, 01/10/15 Initial release. als 2.2 als 02/01/16 Functions with pointer arguments that don't modify contents now const. Added ability to insert a custom video timing table. yh Added 3D support. 3.0 aad 05/13/16 Added API to search for RB video modes. 3.1 rco 07/26/16 Added extern definition for timing table array Added video-in-memory color formats Updated XVidC_RegisterCustomTimingModes API signature 4.1 rco 11/23/16 Added new memory formats Added new API to get video mode id that matches exactly with provided timing information Fix c++ warnings 4.2 jsr 07/22/17 Added new framerates and color formats to support SDI Reordered YCBCR422 colorforamt and removed other formats that are not needed for SDI which were added earlier. vyc 10/04/17 Added new streaming alpha formats and new memory formats 4.3 eb 26/01/18 Added API XVidC_GetVideoModeIdExtensive jsr 02/22/18 Added XVIDC_CSF_YCBCR_420 color space format vyc 04/04/18 Added BGR8 memory format

detaileddescription.para: All content in this file is agnostic of communication interface protocol.

simplesect.para: None.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 11/09/14 Initial release. 2.2 als 02/01/16 Functions with pointer arguments that don't modify contents now const. 4.0 aad 10/26/16 Added API for colormetry which returns fixed point in Q0.10 format instead of float.

detaileddescription.para: All content in this file is agnostic of communication interface protocol.

simplesect.para: None.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 11/09/14 Initial release. 2.2 als 02/01/16 Functions with pointer arguments that don't modify contents now const. 4.0 aad 10/26/16 Functions which return fixed point values instead of float

simplesect.para: None.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als, 01/10/15 Initial release. rc 2.0 als 08/14/15 Added new video timings. 2.1 als 11/04/15 Fixed video timings for some resolutions. rco 02/09/17 Fix c++ compilation warnings 4.2 jsr 07/08/17 Added new video timings for SDI supported resolutions aad 07/10/17 Add XVIDC_VM_3840x2160_60_P_RB video format aad 09/05/17 Fixed timings for 1366x768_60_P aad 09/05/17 Added 1366x768_60_P_RB 4.4 eb 09/04/18 Fixed timings for 1280x720_24_P, 1280x720_25_P, 1280x720_30_P 4.5 jsr 07/03/18 Added timing for new video mode XVIDC_VM_720x486_60_I 4.6 mmo 02/14/19 Added 5k, 8k, 10k and Low Resolution with 200Hz, 240Hz 4.12 kp 15/07/21 Added new video timing modes kp 24/08/21 Corrected the vpolarity, vfreq and vactive kp 24/08/21 Added new video timing modes
